title: 2003 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of Seafarers - Disciplinary Offenses and Procedures
content: This section enumerates pre-employment offenses and offenses during employment for seafarers under the 2003 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of Seafarers. It details specific offenses such as submission of false documents, desertion, absence without leave, insubordination, drunkenness, gambling, violation of company policies, incompetency, grave abuse of authority, and other gross misbehaviors. For each offense, penalties are prescribed ranging from suspension periods (six months to several years) to delisting from the POEA Registry. The rules also specify procedures for filing complaints, contents and form of complaints, exempting circumstances for refusal or abandonment of employment, temporary disqualification from maritime employment during pending complaints, effect of filing an answer, disqualification upon final order, and preventive suspension when evidence is strong and offense serious. The offenses and penalties aim to maintain discipline and good order among seafarers in overseas employment programs.
